summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar 1973 performance with soaring Jerry solos and magical Dark Star/Playin' jams.
review: The recording is an outstanding AUD‑tape soundboard source that sounds crisp, balanced and virtually flawless.  The show itself is praised as one of the band’s finest mid‑summer ’73 outings, highlighted by inspired guitar work, tight rhythm section interplay and memorable renditions of Playin', Dark Star, Eyes of the World, Bird Song and a host of classic Americana numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers are highly enthusiastic (5‑star average ≈ 4.7), noting both superb soundboard quality and an exceptional musical performance.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crisp, incredible, and faithful to the original AUD tape taken directly from the board; no noted drop‑outs or noise issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid first set that builds into a soaring second set; the opener “The Greatest Story Ever Told” kicks things off, followed by tightly woven transitions into Dark Star, Ramble On Rose and a heartfelt close with Stella Blue, Sugar Magnolia and One More Saturday Night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring solos, perfect tone, emotive harmonics and overall inspired playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for dropping “bombs,” delivering syncopated intensity on bass and sharing spotlight during jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Billy Kreutzmann’s drumming is highlighted as being in the zone, driving the jams and weaving a tight rhythmic tapestry.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano is described as twinkling, melding perfectly with Jerry’s guitar and adding delicate texture to the improvisations.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work (Billy) receives strong commendation for its energy and seamless integration with the band.
